metadata.summary: Build partner-driven acquisition channels where publishers earn commissions on referrals. Program design, recruitment, enablement, tracking, fraud prevention, and payouts.

content.title: Growth Affiliate Marketing
content.summary: A structured approach to building partner-driven acquisition channels where third-party publishers earn commissions on sales they refer. Covers program design (commission structure, cookie duration, terms), affiliate recruitment, asset library creation, onboarding sequences, performance tracking, fraud prevention, and payout operations.

why.p: Linear ad spend produces linear growth; affiliates create leverage — when incentive structure and asset quality align, a small roster of quality partners compounds new customer acquisition without proportional ad budget increases. Commission capped at less than 30% of LTV produces positive unit economics while motivating partners.

list.item: Recurring-revenue product (SaaS, subscription, course) with LTV high enough to support 20-40% commissions.
Digital product with self-serve checkout and clean link/code attribution.
Niche ICP reachable through 20-50 quality newsletter writers, bloggers, or course creators.
Checkout is Stripe/Paddle/Lemon Squeezy-compatible (affiliate link or discount-code friendly).
Sub-$30 transactional products with thin margin — payouts + admin overhead exceed return.
Pre-PMF or volatile pricing — unstable offers burn affiliate relationships.
Brand-sensitive regulated verticals (finance, medical) — affiliate copy creates compliance risk.
No asset library exists yet — affiliates without swipes/banners won't promote.
Marketplace price-comparison products — coupon-site affiliates cannibalize organic conversions.

rule.statement: Cap total commission at less than 30% of 12-month LTV; anything above makes the program net-loss when factoring in platform and admin costs.
rule.rationale: Commission must be sustainable at scale. A 40% payout on a $99 LTV product produces a slow bleed as the affiliate roster grows.

rule.statement: Choose the commission model based on product type: percentage-of-sale for digital subscriptions, flat fee for high-ticket services, recurring (lifetime) for SaaS with high LTV, tiered for motivating top performers.
rule.rationale: Recurring commissions on SaaS align affiliate incentives with retention; flat fees suit high-ticket or services where LTV is predictable but average order value is high.

rule.statement: Set cookie duration to 30-90 days; use last-touch attribution by default and document it explicitly in affiliate terms.
rule.rationale: Cookie duration below 30 days loses affiliates who produce delayed conversions from content like blog posts. Attribution model must be declared up front to avoid disputes.

rule.statement: Delay payouts 30-60 days after the sale to cover the refund window; pay outstanding commissions on program termination.
rule.rationale: Paying out before the refund window closes loses money on cancellations. Reliable on-schedule payment is the top factor affiliates use to rate a program.

rule.statement: Set a minimum payout threshold ($50-100) and pay monthly on a fixed schedule.
rule.rationale: Small payouts create processing overhead. A predictable schedule builds trust; erratic payments cause affiliate churn.

rule.statement: Explicitly prohibit brand-keyword PPC bidding, coupon-site listing, spam/unsolicited email, and incentivized traffic in program terms; monitor for violations and enforce termination.
rule.rationale: Coupon-site and brand-keyword affiliates cannibalize cheap organic conversions. Spam traffic violates FTC endorsement rules and can produce chargebacks.

rule.statement: Flag transactions where the same IP records more than 3 sales in 1 hour, click-to-conversion time is under 30 seconds, or referrer is a known coupon domain.
rule.rationale: These patterns indicate click stuffing or transaction injection. Automated fraud sweeps before payout approval prevent irreversible losses.

antipattern.description: Two-tier (MLM-style) commission structures where affiliates earn on recruits' sales.
antipattern.reason: Attracts low-quality recruiters; creates FTC compliance risk and tax-form complexity with no proportional lift in quality referrals.

rule.statement: Backstop cookie-only attribution with promo codes and logged-in-user attribution to recover the 20-40% of conversions lost to iOS 14.5, ITP, and ad-blockers.
rule.rationale: Cookie-only tracking understates affiliate contribution and underpays honest affiliates while overpaying last-click coupon sites that inject themselves after attribution is broken.

section.name: Affiliate Types

rule.statement: Prioritize niche newsletter writers as first-recruit targets; they typically offer the highest CPM-equivalent ROI versus broad bloggers because their audience trust is higher and topic alignment is tighter.
rule.rationale: Newsletter writers have direct inbox access to a pre-qualified audience. Open rates above 25% with prior sponsored content signal monetization willingness and audience trust.

section.p: High-value types in order of typical effort-to-revenue: newsletter writers (low effort, targeted), course creators (low effort, curriculum integration), power users/existing customers (low effort, authentic voice), comparison sites (medium effort, high intent traffic), content bloggers (medium effort, SEO value), agencies recommending to clients (medium effort, B2B).

rule.statement: Personalize outreach with a specific reference to the affiliate's recent content before mentioning the program; generic "partnership opportunity" emails get ignored.
rule.rationale: Affiliates receive mass outreach constantly. A specific reference proves you are a real reader and increases reply rate significantly.

rule.statement: State the commission structure and average monthly earnings range in the first outreach message; affiliates will not reply to vague "great opportunity" messages.
rule.rationale: Time is the affiliate's constraint. Concrete numbers allow instant triage. "Our top affiliates earn $X/month" backed by real distribution data also sets honest expectations.

rule.statement: Provide at minimum: unique tracking links, banner ads (multiple sizes), email swipe copy, social post copy, demo video, and case studies before recruiting affiliates.
rule.rationale: Affiliates promote what is easy to use. Without assets, even motivated affiliates stall. The asset library quality is the single biggest predictor of activation rate.

antipattern.description: Launching the program without an asset library and expecting affiliates to create their own promotional materials.
antipattern.reason: Affiliates abandon programs that require significant creative work; they move to programs that make promotion frictionless.

rule.statement: Run a structured onboarding sequence: Day 0 welcome + credentials, Day 1 dashboard tutorial, Day 3 best-performing strategies, Day 7 check-in, Day 14 first-sale tips, Day 30 recap.
rule.rationale: 80% of affiliates who don't produce a sale in the first 30 days never will. A structured onboarding shortens time-to-first-sale and increases the activation rate above 30%.

rule.statement: Provide a "top 10 starter playbook" covering the highest-converting tactics from existing affiliates so new partners can copy proven approaches immediately.
rule.rationale: New affiliates lack context on what messaging and channels work. Sharing winning tactics reduces experimentation time and aligns affiliate copy with brand standards.

rule.statement: Recompute LTV quarterly and adjust commission tiers accordingly; never let affiliates discover a commission cut mid-campaign.
rule.rationale: Affiliates plan promotional calendars around commission rates. Surprise cuts destroy trust and trigger public complaints in affiliate communities.

section.name: Affiliate Tracking Metrics and Real Examples
Key Metrics

rule.statement: Track five program-level metrics weekly: affiliate activation rate (target above 30%), conversion rate (target above 2%), revenue per active affiliate, CAC via affiliates (must stay below 30% of LTV), and fraud rate (target below 1%).
rule.rationale: These five metrics together diagnose whether the program is healthy. Low activation rate means recruitment or onboarding failure; high CAC means commission is too high or traffic quality is low.

section.p: Affiliate activation rate = affiliates with 1+ referral / total affiliates. Conversion rate = sales / clicks. CAC via affiliates = total commissions paid / customers acquired. Fraud rate = flagged transactions / total transactions.

example.title: SaaS Affiliate Program
example.p: Project management tool. Structure: 30% recurring commission, 60-day cookie, monthly payouts at $50 minimum. Recruited 50 productivity bloggers, 15 joined, 5 became active promoters. Results over 12 months: 200 customers, $60K ARR attributed, $18K commissions paid. Effective affiliate CAC: $90 vs $150 via paid ads — 40% more efficient.

example.title: Course Affiliate Program
example.p: $500 online course. Structure: 40% per sale ($200 flat), 30-day cookie. Targeted newsletter writers in niche, created co-branded landing pages, provided exclusive bonuses. Results: 50 affiliates, 300 sales ($150K revenue), $60K commissions paid — 10x ROI on program management cost.

antipattern.description: Inactive affiliate tail: signing up many affiliates without a pruning process.
antipattern.reason: 80% of affiliates produce zero sales. An unpruned roster inflates support overhead and dilutes program resources. Healthy programs continually recruit quality partners and prune inactive ones quarterly.

antipattern.description: Paying commissions before the refund window closes.
antipattern.reason: Cancellations and chargebacks that arrive after payout are unrecoverable losses. The 30-day holdback is non-negotiable arithmetic, not a courtesy.

section.name: Tools Reference
section.p: Platform options: Rewardful (Stripe-native SaaS), FirstPromoter (SaaS-focused), PartnerStack (B2B deeper PRM), Lemon Squeezy and Paddle (built-in affiliate for digital products). Fraud detection: pandas for transaction pattern analysis, playwright for brand-keyword PPC monitoring. Payouts: Wise or PayPal Mass-Pay for cross-border.

template.purpose: Key-points affiliate agreement template with commission, rules, termination, liability
Welcome email with link, dashboard access, quick-start steps, and asset links
Monthly payout calculation with refund holdback (30-day window)
